catalog_card.title: Jeju Cruise Shore: Small-Group Tour
catalog_card.subtitle: Port-based cruise day tour with Jeju and Gangjeong route options
catalog_card.shortCardDescription: A small group tour cruise shore excursion that adapts the route to your cruise port. Jeju Port (north) runs an east-coast UNESCO route via Seongsan Ilchulbong; Gangjeong Port (south) runs a Hallasan 1100 + south-coast geology + Olle Market route. Both return to the cruise terminal with comfortable buffer.

hero.tagline: Pick your cruise port: Jeju Port runs the east UNESCO route, Gangjeong Port runs the Hallasan + Olle Market route. Both 8-hour routes run on a small-group mini-coach.

itineraryStops.name: Jeju Cruise Terminal Pickup (Jeju Port — north)
itineraryStops.description:
Your driver and English-speaking guide meet your party at the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널, address: 191 Imhang-ro, Jeju-si — UN/LOCODE **KRCHA**), the northern coast cruise port that opened in **2015** to receive international cruise vessels. 

The terminal has two berths — the **Main Berth (360 m, 12 m water depth, 140,000-ton capacity)** and an **Emergency Berth (306 m, 11.5 m water depth, 85,000-ton capacity)** — handling **one cruise ship at a time**. Pickup is timed for **30 minutes after your ship's posted clearance**, absorbing immigration variance during multi-ship arrivals. 

The driver carries an English/Korean name placard, a printed itinerary card (with all stop times and Korean-script addresses for emergency taxi use), and the operator's 24/7 contact number. Vehicle is parked in the **100-bus parking capacity lot** adjacent to the arrival hall. 

Before boarding, the guide reviews the day's flow, all-aboard time, weather, and any port-day adjustments. If your guests need currency exchange (Jeju Bank), a SIM/Wi-Fi rental, or use of the multilingual tourist information center, the guide accompanies — these are all on-site within the terminal. 

Per **South Korea's Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism**, **810,000 cruise tourists arrived on 414 ships in 2024** — nearly tripling the prior year — so terminal congestion is realistic on multi-ship days.
highlights: **Pickup 30 min after ship clearance** — buffer absorbs immigration delays
Driver + English-speaking guide meet at arrival hall with name placard
**Main Berth 360 m × 12 m / 140,000-ton capacity** (single concurrent ship)
**100-bus parking** capacity adjacent to terminal — fast vehicle boarding
Tourist info center, Jeju Bank currency exchange, ATMs, free Wi-Fi on-site
Itinerary card with Korean-script addresses provided as emergency-taxi backup

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (Jeju cruise terminal facilities + 100-bus parking)
South Korea Ministry of Oceans & Fisheries (terminal opening 2015; berth specifications)
Korea Cruise Port Sales / k-cruiseinfo.com (berth depths, tonnage capacity)
Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism (2024 cruise volume: 810,000 pax / 414 ships)
Jeju Provincial Government (Imhang-ro address, Geonip-dong; UN/LOCODE KRCHA)

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Small-group cruise pickup** at the Jeju International Passenger Terminal arrival hall gives the guide time to confirm guest names, walking comfort, photo priorities, and any key requests before departure. The smaller vehicle and group size make boarding simpler than a large coach and give the route more flexibility if ship clearance or weather changes. The 30-minute post-clearance buffer is kept so the group can start calmly without putting the ship-return timing at risk.

smartNotes.tip: Arrive 5–10 minutes before scheduled pickup; bring photo ID for cruise/DMZ tours

convenience.restroom: At meeting point (subway station / hotel lobby / port terminal)

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. 

The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. 

**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. 

**Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run once daily at 14:00 (1 show/day) at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. 

Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. 

**Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: **Triple UNESCO**: World Heritage 2007-07-02 + Biosphere Reserve 2002 + Global Geopark 2010-10
**5,000 years old** — Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic eruption, magma-meets-seawater steam-explosive birth
Crater **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** (the 'stone crown')
**182 m above sea level** — 0.8 km / ~140 m gain, 60-min round trip on stone-step trail
**18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** at base — 1945 kamikaze suicide-boat shelters
**Haenyeo demonstration once daily at 14:00 (subject to bad-weather cancellation)** at the seaside cove — UNESCO Intangible 2016
**Admission updated April 2025**: Adult ₩5,000 / Youth ₩2,500 / FREE under 7 + over 65
Spring/autumn hours **05:00–19:00** (last entry 18:20)
**Closes for first Monday of each month** (occasional rest day)
timeUsed: Ascent to summit deck (0.8 km / 140 m gain) — ~30 min
Summit views + crater rim photos — ~20 min
Optional haenyeo viewing at base (14:00 show; may be canceled in bad weather) — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Seongsan Ilchulbong is the main natural highlight of the east route.** The small-group format gives guests more time for photos, questions, and pace adjustments on the crater trail. The guide can explain the 5,000-year-old volcanic formation, the sea views, and the haenyeo culture around the base without rushing the group. If conditions or guest mobility make the full climb unsuitable, the visit can still work well from the lower viewing areas.

itineraryStops.name: Seopjikoji (East Cape)
itineraryStops.category: Volcanic Cape — Tadao Ando Architecture + Bangdupo Lighthouse + Drama Filming
itineraryStops.description:
**Seopjikoji (섭지코지)** is a narrow volcanic cape on Jeju's east coast. In Jeju dialect, **koji** means a cape or headland, and the place is known for open sea views, a gentle coastal path, the white lighthouse, and the broad view back toward Seongsan Ilchulbong. 

The walk is scenic without being a heavy hike, so it works well for cruise guests who want strong photos and fresh sea air without spending the whole day climbing. Depending on time, guests may also see the Glass House area and the Yumin Art Museum exterior, both associated with architect Tadao Ando. 

The visit is kept flexible because wind, rain, gallery hours, and group walking speed can affect how much of the cape is covered.
highlights: **2.1 km cape trail** (~30 min, only 14 m gain) — wheelchair/stroller-friendly stretches
**Bangdupo Lighthouse** — octagonal, **flashes every 4 sec**, atop Bulgeun Oreum red volcanic cone
**Hyeopja Yeondae Beacon Fire Station** — 4 m × 9 m Joseon yeondae, **Special Monument No. 23-2**
**Tadao Ando architecture** — Glass House restaurant + Yumin Art Gallery (Phoenix Island)
**Seonbawi (Seon Rock)** — 10 m offshore volcanic neck, dragon king's youngest son legend
**Drama filming**: 'All In' (2003), 'My Girl' (2005), and others
**Canola season March–April** (yellow slopes); **hydrangea June** (blue/violet)
**FREE cape admission**; Yumin Art Gallery + Glass House separately ticketed
Seongsan Ilchulbong **clearly visible 4 km north** from the cape tip — perfect Triple-UNESCO panorama backdrop
timeUsed: Tadao Ando buildings exterior viewing — ~10 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Seopjikoji works especially well in a small group** because the pace can be adjusted for wind, photos, coffee, or a short gallery stop when time and opening conditions allow. The coastal path gives open views toward Seongsan Ilchulbong, while the lighthouse and Tadao Ando-related architecture add a clean visual contrast to the volcanic scenery. The guide can keep the visit focused on the parts guests care about most instead of moving everyone through at a fixed coach-tour pace.

smartNotes.photo: The lighthouse with cliff backdrop is iconic; canola fields with Seongsan Ilchulbong in distance is the spring signature shot
smartNotes.facilities: Aqua Planet Jeju aquarium and several cafés on site
smartNotes.tip: Combine with Seongsan Ilchulbong visit (10 min drive); spring (canola) and autumn (eulalia grass) are peak photo seasons

convenience.restroom: Available at parking lot and Aqua Planet area

visitBasics.walking: Easy to moderate — flat coastal walkway; total loop ≈ 1 hour

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (East Jeju — local seafood or black pork)
itineraryStops.description:
**East Jeju lunch stop** at a guide-vetted local restaurant in the Seongsan / Seongeup / Pyoseon area, chosen to fit cruise day-trip timing. 

**Two typical options**, selected based on group preference and availability: **(1) Jeju Black Pork Heukdwaeji BBQ** (제주 흑돼지) — Jeju's signature ingredient, the indigenous Black Pig was designated **Natural Monument of Korea** in 2015, and pork-belly cuts are grilled over charcoal at the table with traditional banchan; or **(2) Galchi Jorim** (갈치조림 — braised hairtail with radish, soy-chili sauce) — eastern Jeju's famous seafood dish, drawing on the daily catch from Seongsan and Seongeup-area harbors. 

Vegetarian options are available at most venues with advance notice (the guide confirms with the restaurant ~30 min ahead). Lunch duration is **~60 minutes**, fitting the cruise day-trip's tight timeline (full day usually 8 hours port-to-port). Beverages: barley tea (free), local **Jeju Beer** (~₩6,000), or **Hallasan Soju** (Jeju's signature distilled spirit, ~₩5,000). 

All restaurants in the rotation accept **major cards (Visa/Mastercard) + cash KRW**; bilingual photo menus (English/Chinese) are standard at the cruise-tour-vetted venues. Lunch is **NOT included** in the standard cruise tour pricing — typical out-of-pocket is **₩15,000–25,000 per person** (varies by dish); inclusive lunch upgrade available on booking.
highlights: Guide-vetted East Jeju restaurant — Seongsan / Seongeup / Pyoseon area
**Jeju Black Pork BBQ** (Natural Monument 2015) or **Galchi Jorim** (braised hairtail)
~60 min duration — fits cruise day-trip timeline
Bilingual photo menus (English/Chinese), card + cash accepted
Vegetarian options on advance notice
**Lunch NOT included** — out-of-pocket ₩15,000–25,000/pax (upgrade available)

sources: Jeju Black Pig Natural Monument designation (2015, Cultural Heritage Administration)

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The small-group lunch stop gives guests a calmer mid-day break.** A guide-vetted East Jeju restaurant is chosen based on the day's timing, group preference, and opening conditions. The guide can help with menu translation and local dish recommendations, including black pork, seafood, or vegetarian-friendly options when arranged in advance. The longer lunch pace also gives the route a useful buffer if the morning stops take more time for photos or walking.

smartNotes.tip: Inform guide of any dietary restrictions (vegetarian, halal, allergies) at booking; cash and major cards typically accepted

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
itineraryStops.description:
**Seongeup Folk Village (성읍민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. 

Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju household culture, stone grandfather statues, and local stories connected to village life.
highlights: **Founded 1423** by Sejong the Great — administrative center until **1914 (~500 years)**
**110 traditional structures** within original eupseong walled-town footprint
**Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea** (entire village + multiple buildings)
**Two ~600-year-old trees** at center: Saw-leaf Zelkova + Japanese Hackberry — **Natural Monuments**
Designed on **Joseon Korean feng shui (geomancy) principles** — typical county-capital plan
**'Dae Jang Geum' (MBC 2003–2004)** filming location — international K-drama hit
**Omegisul (오메기술)** — rice-based Jeju liquor brewed by single family for generations (free tasting)
Historical scars: **1826 fire** destroyed ~80 buildings; **1948 Jeju Uprising** damage
**FREE admission** — village still inhabited by ~250 families maintaining traditions
timeUsed: Parking + welcome brief by village docent — ~10 min
Walk to ancient trees + village center — ~10 min
Hanok interior visits (2–3 representative homes) — ~15 min
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Seongeup Folk Village is the main cultural stop of the east route.** In a small group, the guide can explain Jeju stone houses, old village lanes, local household customs, and the role this area played in Joseon-era administration at a more personal pace. Access to private homes or resident-led explanations depends on local conditions, so the visit is presented as a respectful village walk rather than a promised home-entry program.

sources: Cultural Heritage Administration — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage designation
Korea Cultural Heritage Foundation — Seongeup Folk Village historical records
VisitJeju — Seongeup Minsok Maeul
MBC Drama Archive — Dae Jang Geum (2003–2004) filming locations
Jeju Folklore Society — Omegisul brewing tradition documentation

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls (dolhareubang grandfathers), thatched-roof houses with smoke holes, and traditional doors are iconic Jeju shots
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

visitBasics.admission: Free for general village; some private homes charge for guided visits
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat dirt and stone paths through the village

itineraryStops.name: Return to Jeju Cruise Terminal (Jeju Port — north)
itineraryStops.description:
Return drive to the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널) on Jeju City's northern coast. The route from East Jeju (Seongsan/Seongeup area) takes ~70–80 minutes via Highway 1132 (the coastal ring road) and Route 16, with one designated comfort stop. Drop-off is timed to deliver guests to the terminal **at least 60 minutes before your ship's all-aboard time**, traveler-fit the cruise industry's standard buffer for embarkation queues. 

The vehicle pulls into the **100-bus parking capacity lot**, guests disembark at the terminal entrance with luggage assistance from the driver/guide. If guests have early evening flights from Jeju International Airport, **Bus #100 connects directly from the cruise terminal to the airport** — the guide can advise on this option. 

The terminal interior offers convenience store, pharmacy, snack shop, souvenir shops with **tax-free items**, Jeju Bank ATM accepting overseas cards, currency exchange (typical hours 08:00–17:00), and SK Telecom T-mobile Wi-Fi rental counter — useful if guests want last-minute purchases or to reload mobile data before sailaway. 

**Free shuttle bus to Dongmun Traditional Market** (oldest/largest in Jeju City, ~10 min) is also available for any guests with extra time before all-aboard.
highlights: **Drop-off 60+ min before all-aboard time** — standard cruise embarkation buffer
~70–80 min from East Jeju via Highway 1132 + Route 16 with one comfort stop
Driver/guide assist with luggage at terminal entrance
Bus #100 connects terminal directly to **Jeju International Airport** (for late flights)
Tax-free souvenir shops, Jeju Bank ATM, currency exchange, Wi-Fi rental on-site
Free shuttle to **Dongmun Traditional Market** (~10 min) if extra time before all-aboard

sources: VisitKorea / english.visitkorea.or.kr (Jeju cruise terminal facilities)
Jeju Bus Information System (Bus #100 airport connection)
Korea Cruise Port Sales (terminal layout + drop-off zones)
VisitJeju.net (Dongmun Market free shuttle)
Cruise industry standard (60-min all-aboard buffer)

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The return drive is kept conversational and flexible.** The guide uses the drive back to answer remaining questions, summarize the day's volcanic and cultural highlights, and monitor timing for the ship. The schedule is planned to return to Jeju Port with a comfortable buffer before all-aboard, with pace adjustments made if traffic or weather changes.

smartNotes.tip: Confirm exact drop-off location with guide before journey begins; cruise tours coordinate with ship departure schedule

visitBasics.hours: Per drop-off schedule (see hero / glanceItems for return time band)

stops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
stops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
stops.description: **Seongeup Folk Village (성읍민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju household culture, stone grandfather statues, and local stories connected to village life.
highlights: **Founded 1423** by Sejong the Great — administrative center until **1914 (~500 years)**
**110 traditional structures** within original eupseong walled-town footprint
**Important Folklore Cultural Heritage of South Korea** (entire village + multiple buildings)
**Two ~600-year-old trees** at center: Saw-leaf Zelkova + Japanese Hackberry — **Natural Monuments**
Designed on **Joseon Korean feng shui (geomancy) principles** — typical county-capital plan
**'Dae Jang Geum' (MBC 2003–2004)** filming location — international K-drama hit
**Omegisul (오메기술)** — rice-based Jeju liquor brewed by single family for generations (free tasting)
Historical scars: **1826 fire** destroyed ~80 buildings; **1948 Jeju Uprising** damage
**FREE admission** — village still inhabited by ~250 families maintaining traditions
timeUsed: Parking + welcome brief by village docent — ~10 min
Walk to ancient trees + village center — ~10 min
Hanok interior visits (2–3 representative homes) — ~15 min
stops.whyOnRoute: **Seongeup Folk Village is the main cultural stop of the east route.** In a small group, the guide can explain Jeju stone houses, old village lanes, local household customs, and the role this area played in Joseon-era administration at a more personal pace. Access to private homes or resident-led explanations depends on local conditions, so the visit is presented as a respectful village walk rather than a promised home-entry program.

stops.name: Return to Jeju Cruise Terminal (Jeju Port — north)
stops.description: Return drive to the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널) on Jeju City's northern coast. The route from East Jeju (Seongsan/Seongeup area) takes ~70–80 minutes via Highway 1132 (the coastal ring road) and Route 16, with one designated comfort stop. Drop-off is timed to deliver guests to the terminal **at least 60 minutes before your ship's all-aboard time**, traveler-fit the cruise industry's standard buffer for embarkation queues. The vehicle pulls into the **100-bus parking capacity lot**, guests disembark at the terminal entrance with luggage assistance from the driver/guide. If guests have early evening flights from Jeju International Airport, **Bus #100 connects directly from the cruise terminal to the airport** — the guide can advise on this option. The terminal interior offers convenience store, pharmacy, snack shop, souvenir shops with **tax-free items**, Jeju Bank ATM accepting overseas cards, currency exchange (typical hours 08:00–17:00), and SK Telecom T-mobile Wi-Fi rental counter — useful if guests want last-minute purchases or to reload mobile data before sailaway. **Free shuttle bus to Dongmun Traditional Market** (oldest/largest in Jeju City, ~10 min) is also available for any guests with extra time before all-aboard.

stops.name: Hallasan 1100 Altitude Wetland (Ramsar + UNESCO)
stops.category: Alpine Wetland — Ramsar Site + within Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage
stops.description: **Hallasan 1100 Altitude Wetland (한라산 1100고지 습지)** is a high-elevation alpine wetland system on the Hallasan plateau, located **at exactly 1,100 m above sea level** along **Route 1100 — the highest national highway in Korea** linking Jeju City to Seogwipo Jungmun. The site is a **Ramsar Wetland of International Importance (Site No. 1893, designated 12 October 2009)**, falling within the **Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage Site (2007)**, **Jeju Island UNESCO Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Hallasan National Park (1970)** — making this one of the few sites globally with **four overlapping international/national designations**. The wetland comprises **16+ discontinuous freshwater marshes and pools** spread across a low-gradient plateau section that captures and retains rainwater, forming **small permanent lakes and peat bogs** that support diverse floristic assemblages with significant Jeju endemism. Recorded biodiversity includes **3 threatened bird species and 1 threatened plant species within Korea**, plus mammal, reptile, and insect species endemic to Jeju Island. The site received **900,000 visitors in 2008** (Mt Halla Visitor Centre data). Visitor experience: a **1-km wooden boardwalk loop** with observation decks, interpretive signage, and a small natural-history exhibit at the trailhead. **Autumn foliage (October–November)** and **winter snow scenes (December–February)** are the most photographed seasons; **spring + summer** offer wetland flora and endemic species visibility. **FREE admission**; adjacent **1100 Rest Area (1100 휴게소)** provides indoor restrooms + cafe + small souvenir shop. **Cultural and religious importance** to local communities is documented in the Ramsar listing — historical shaman pilgrimages tied to mountain-water spirits.

**1,100 m above sea level** — on Korea's **highest national highway** (Route 1100)

**16+ discontinuous wetlands** — freshwater marshes + pools + peat bogs

The area is much cooler than the coast, so it creates a memorable contrast before the route descends toward the Jungmun and Seogwipo coastline.

stops.name: Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff
stops.description: **Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff (대포 주상절리)** is one of Jeju's clearest examples of columnar basalt along the coast. Lava from Hallasan-area volcanic activity cooled and contracted into tightly packed polygonal stone columns, creating the dramatic cliff line now viewed from a safe seaside deck. The columns rise above the waves on the Jungmun coast and are especially photogenic when the sea is active. The visit is short, high-impact, and easier than a long coastal hike, making it a strong stop for mixed-age groups. Walking is mostly on maintained paths and viewing platforms, but wind and sea spray can vary by weather and tide.
highlights: **Natural Monument No. 443** — designated **2005-01-06**, the most recent Jeju coastal NM
Columns rise **30–40 m**, packed in **5- to 7-sided polygons** (mostly hexagons) along **1 km** of coastline
Formation: **Hallasan flank lava cooled in the sea ~250,000–140,000 years ago**, contracting perpendicular to cooling face
**East Asia's premier columnar-jointing site** — peer to Giant's Causeway, Devils Postpile, Fingal's Cave
Wave-spray photos may be possible when the tide and wind conditions are right
**0.8 km boardwalk loop**, ~15 min walk — barrier-free deck, wheelchair/stroller accessible
Part of **Jeju Global Geopark (UNESCO 2010-10)** — 13 island-wide geosites
Admission **₩2,000 adult / ₩1,000 youth / FREE under 7 + over 65**
Hallasan summit visible to the north on clear days, **directly downstream** geologically
timeUsed: Walk to first viewing platform — ~5 min
stops.whyOnRoute: **Daepo Jusangjeolli Cliff is a compact, high-impact geology stop.** The viewing deck gives guests a clear look at Jeju's columnar basalt cliffs without a long hike, and the site fits naturally after Hallasan 1100 Highland before the route continues toward Seogwipo. The guide may adjust photo time depending on wind, tide, and crowd levels.

stops.name: Cheonjiyeon Falls (Seogwipo old-town waterfall)
stops.description: **Cheonjiyeon Falls** (천지연 폭포 — literally 'Sky-meeting-Land Falls') is the closer-to-port of Seogwipo's two famous waterfalls and is **NOT to be confused with Cheonjeyeon Falls** (천제연 — the three-tier 'Pond of the Heavenly Emperor' falls in Jungmun, ~15 min west). The single-tier cascade plunges **22 m / 12 m wide into a 20 m-deep emerald pool** at the end of a **gentle 1 km riverside trail** (~15 min one-way, paved and stroller-friendly) that follows a subtropical gorge dense with **rare plants**: **Damselfly Tree** (Natural Monument No. 163), **Crepidomanes makinoi** ferns clinging to the basalt walls, and dozens of endemic species protected as **Natural Monument No. 379** (the falls + surrounding habitat ensemble). The pool below is the protected habitat of the **Mutae eel** (마부태 / 무태 — *Anguilla marmorata*, the marbled eel), itself **Natural Monument No. 27** — a nocturnal species whose movement under nighttime illumination is one of Cheonjiyeon's signature scenes. Admission is **₩2,000 adults / ₩1,000 children** (one of Jeju's most affordable nature stops); operating hours **09:00–22:00 (last entry 21:30)** — the **after-dark illumination program 19:00–22:00** is unique among Jeju waterfalls and turns the gorge walls + falls into an emerald-jade light show, often paired with cruise overnight stays. The trailhead is **a 5-minute walk from Olle Maeil Market**, making the two stops a natural pairing on cruise day-trips originating from Gangjeong port. On clear days, **Munseom Island** (3 km offshore — Korea's first marine-park designation, 1994) is visible from the falls observation deck, and the basalt cliff faces of the gorge expose the same Hallasan-flank lava-flow stratigraphy as Daepo Jusangjeolli.
highlights: **Single-tier cascade 22 m × 12 m into 20 m-deep emerald pool** — Seogwipo's signature waterfall
**NOT Cheonjeyeon (천제연)** — different falls, different gorge, ~15 min west in Jungmun
**Natural Monument No. 379** (falls + endemic-flora habitat ensemble)
**Mutae eel (Natural Monument No. 27)** habitat — nocturnal marbled eel under illumination
**Damselfly Tree (Natural Monument No. 163)** + Crepidomanes makinoi ferns line the gorge
Gentle **1 km paved trail (~15 min)** — stroller/wheelchair friendly
**19:00–22:00 night illumination** — unique among Jeju waterfalls
**5-min walk from Olle Maeil Market** — natural pairing for south-route cruise days

stops.name: Olle Maeil Market (Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market)
stops.description: **Seogwipo Maeil Olle Market (서귀포매일올레시장)** is the largest traditional market in Seogwipo and a convenient food-and-shopping stop near Gangjeong Port. The market began in the early 1960s and now stretches through a covered arcade with many local food stalls, produce shops, seafood vendors, bakeries, and souvenir stands. Guests can look for Jeju citrus, black pork snacks, seafood, omegi rice cakes, local sweets, and simple gifts to take back to the ship. Most visitors enjoy it as a lively local-market walk rather than a formal shopping stop. Payment methods vary by stall, so carrying some Korean won is still recommended even though many larger shops accept cards.

staticQuestions.question: How do I know which port my cruise calls at?
staticQuestions.answer: Your cruise booking confirmation lists the Jeju port. We confirm the port with you at booking and route the day accordingly. Most major cruise lines call at Jeju Port (north terminal); some Seogwipo-area calls use Gangjeong Port.

staticQuestions.question: Will I be back to the ship in time?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes. Both route options return to the cruise terminal between 16:30-17:00, with buffer for any traffic delay. We track your sailaway time and adjust pace if conditions change.

staticQuestions.question: What if my cruise call port changes?
staticQuestions.answer: Notify us as soon as you know — we switch the route option to match. Port-routing is the whole reason this tour has two route options instead of one.

staticQuestions.question: Is this a good choice for first-time visitors to Jeju?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — it covers the most distinctive stops in the area in a single balanced day.

staticQuestions.question: Is the walking difficult?
staticQuestions.answer: Moderate walking with some active sections.

staticQuestions.question: Is this tour suitable for families?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes — comfortable for most families.

staticQuestions.question: Can older adults join comfortably?
staticQuestions.answer: Yes for active seniors; less ideal for low-mobility travelers.

staticQuestions.question: What's the recommended minimum age?
staticQuestions.answer: Best fit is around age 6 and up — children comfortable with a full-day rhythm and the walking notes above.

staticQuestions.question: Can the tour run in rainy weather?
staticQuestions.answer: Several stops are exposed; very wet weather may shorten or substitute stops.

staticQuestions.question: Is lunch included?
staticQuestions.answer: A lunch break is built into the route. Whether the meal itself is included depends on the booked rate — check the inclusions list.

staticQuestions.question: Where do I get picked up?
staticQuestions.answer: At the cruise terminal arrival hall — Jeju Port (north) or Gangjeong Port (south) depending on where your ship docks. Your guide waits at the gangway-side arrival hall with an AtoC Korea sign and your name. Exact pickup time is confirmed the evening before, based on your ship's posted arrival.

hero.tagline: Pick your cruise port: Jeju Port runs the east UNESCO route, Gangjeong Port runs the Hallasan + Olle Market route. Both 8-hour routes run on a small-group mini-coach.

itineraryStops.name: Jeju Cruise Terminal Pickup (Jeju Port — north)
itineraryStops.description: Your driver and English-speaking guide meet your party at the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널, address: 191 Imhang-ro, Jeju-si — UN/LOCODE **KRCHA**), the northern coast cruise port that opened in **2015** to receive international cruise vessels. The terminal has two berths — the **Main Berth (360 m, 12 m water depth, 140,000-ton capacity)** and an **Emergency Berth (306 m, 11.5 m water depth, 85,000-ton capacity)** — handling **one cruise ship at a time**. Pickup is timed for **30 minutes after your ship's posted clearance**, absorbing immigration variance during multi-ship arrivals. The driver carries an English/Korean name placard, a printed itinerary card (with all stop times and Korean-script addresses for emergency taxi use), and the operator's 24/7 contact number. Vehicle is parked in the **100-bus parking capacity lot** adjacent to the arrival hall. Before boarding, the guide reviews the day's flow, all-aboard time, weather, and any port-day adjustments. If your guests need currency exchange (Jeju Bank), a SIM/Wi-Fi rental, or use of the multilingual tourist information center, the guide accompanies — these are all on-site within the terminal. Per **South Korea's Ministry of Culture, Sports & Tourism**, **810,000 cruise tourists arrived on 414 ships in 2024** — nearly tripling the prior year — so terminal congestion is realistic on multi-ship days.
highlights: Direct cruise terminal pickup
English-speaking guide meets at exit

convenience.restroom: At meeting point (subway station / hotel lobby / port terminal)

smartNotes.tip: Arrive 5–10 minutes before scheduled pickup; bring photo ID for cruise/DMZ tours

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Small-group cruise pickup** at the Jeju International Passenger Terminal arrival hall gives the guide time to confirm guest names, walking comfort, photo priorities, and any key requests before departure. The smaller vehicle and group size make boarding simpler than a large coach and give the route more flexibility if ship clearance or weather changes. The 30-minute post-clearance buffer is kept so the group can start calmly without putting the ship-return timing at risk.

itineraryStops.name: Seongsan Ilchulbong
itineraryStops.category: UNESCO Tuff Cone — Sunrise Peak (Triple UNESCO Designation)
itineraryStops.description: **Seongsan Ilchulbong (성산일출봉, 'Sunrise Peak')** is a **5,000-year-old Surtseyan-type hydrovolcanic tuff cone** — a rare landform created when an underwater basalt eruption broke the sea surface, the magma met seawater, and the resulting steam-explosive ash built a **182 m fortress-shaped crater** rim instead of a typical lava cone. The result is what UNESCO formally calls **'an exceptional example of an emergent Surtseyan-type volcanic edifice'**. The crater is **600 m wide × 90 m deep**, ringed by **99 jagged rim rocks** that local lore likens to a stone crown. Seongsan carries the **Triple UNESCO designation**: **World Natural Heritage (inscribed 2007-07-02)** as part of the **'Jeju Volcanic Island and Lava Tubes' property**, **Biosphere Reserve (2002)**, and **Global Geopark (2010-10)** — a stack matched only by Hallasan itself among Korean sites. **18 Imperial Japanese WWII caves** (kamikaze suicide-boat shelters, 1945) are tunneled into the seaward base. Trail: **0.8 km one-way, ~140 m elevation gain, 60 min round trip** on wide stone steps. **Haenyeo (해녀) demonstrations** run once daily at 14:00 (1 show/day) at the rocky cove below the cone — Jeju women divers (UNESCO Intangible Cultural Heritage 2016) descend without scuba gear. Admission updated **April 2025**: **Adult ₩5,000 / Youth+Child ₩2,500 / Group adult ₩1,600**, free under 7 and over 65. The summit deck is also part of the **Yeongjusipgyeong (Jeju 10 Scenic Wonders) canon**, and remains Korea's premier **annual New Year sunrise pilgrimage** destination — 50,000+ visitors ascend before dawn on January 1, with municipal sunrise-festival programming since 1995. **Udo Island (우도, 'Cow Island')** — Korea's largest secondary volcanic island — is clearly visible 4 km offshore from the summit, accessible by **15-min ferry from Seongsan Port** (₩10,500 round-trip) for guests with extra time on a different itinerary.
highlights: UNESCO World Heritage volcanic crater
Optional summit climb to crater rim
Coastal viewpoint at the base

visitBasics.hours: Mar–Apr & Sep–Oct: 05:00–19:00; May–Aug: 04:30–20:00; Nov–Feb: 06:00–18:00 (last ticketing 1 hour before closing)
visitBasics.admission: Adult 5,000 KRW / Youth & Child 2,500 KRW (VisitKorea reference)
visitBasics.walking: Moderate — 20–30 min steady stair climb (≈ 600 stairs); benches every few minutes for rest

smartNotes.tip: All stairs but well-maintained — manageable in casual clothes; family-friendly with rest stops; sunrise visits popular but crowds gather pre-dawn
smartNotes.photo: Summit view of Hallasan to the west and Udo island to the north on clear days; haenyeo black-sand beach view from descending path
smartNotes.facilities: Café (Fritz Coffee 1.3km away has best exterior views of the peak); ferry to Udo Island from Seongsan Port (1.5km/15 min walk)

timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Seongsan Ilchulbong is the main natural highlight of the east route.** The small-group format gives guests more time for photos, questions, and pace adjustments on the crater trail. The guide can explain the 5,000-year-old volcanic formation, the sea views, and the haenyeo culture around the base without rushing the group. If conditions or guest mobility make the full climb unsuitable, the visit can still work well from the lower viewing areas.

itineraryStops.name: Seopjikoji (East Cape)
itineraryStops.category: Volcanic Cape — Tadao Ando Architecture + Bangdupo Lighthouse + Drama Filming
itineraryStops.description: **Seopjikoji (섭지코지)** is a narrow volcanic cape on Jeju's east coast. In Jeju dialect, **koji** means a cape or headland, and the place is known for open sea views, a gentle coastal path, the white lighthouse, and the broad view back toward Seongsan Ilchulbong. The walk is scenic without being a heavy hike, so it works well for cruise guests who want strong photos and fresh sea air without spending the whole day climbing. Depending on time, guests may also see the Glass House area and the Yumin Art Museum exterior, both associated with architect Tadao Ando. The visit is kept flexible because wind, rain, gallery hours, and group walking speed can affect how much of the cape is covered.
highlights: Coastal cliff walk
Lighthouse and chapel viewpoints
K-drama filming locations

visitBasics.walking: Easy to moderate — flat coastal walkway; total loop ≈ 1 hour

convenience.restroom: Available at parking lot and Aqua Planet area

smartNotes.photo: The lighthouse with cliff backdrop is iconic; canola fields with Seongsan Ilchulbong in distance is the spring signature shot
smartNotes.facilities: Aqua Planet Jeju aquarium and several cafés on site
smartNotes.tip: Combine with Seongsan Ilchulbong visit (10 min drive); spring (canola) and autumn (eulalia grass) are peak photo seasons

timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Seopjikoji works especially well in a small group** because the pace can be adjusted for wind, photos, coffee, or a short gallery stop when time and opening conditions allow. The coastal path gives open views toward Seongsan Ilchulbong, while the lighthouse and Tadao Ando-related architecture add a clean visual contrast to the volcanic scenery. The guide can keep the visit focused on the parts guests care about most instead of moving everyone through at a fixed coach-tour pace.

itineraryStops.name: Lunch (East Jeju — local seafood or black pork)
itineraryStops.description: **East Jeju lunch stop** at a guide-vetted local restaurant in the Seongsan / Seongeup / Pyoseon area, chosen to fit cruise day-trip timing. **Two typical options**, selected based on group preference and availability: **(1) Jeju Black Pork Heukdwaeji BBQ** (제주 흑돼지) — Jeju's signature ingredient, the indigenous Black Pig was designated **Natural Monument of Korea** in 2015, and pork-belly cuts are grilled over charcoal at the table with traditional banchan; or **(2) Galchi Jorim** (갈치조림 — braised hairtail with radish, soy-chili sauce) — eastern Jeju's famous seafood dish, drawing on the daily catch from Seongsan and Seongeup-area harbors. Vegetarian options are available at most venues with advance notice (the guide confirms with the restaurant ~30 min ahead). Lunch duration is **~60 minutes**, fitting the cruise day-trip's tight timeline (full day usually 8 hours port-to-port). Beverages: barley tea (free), local **Jeju Beer** (~₩6,000), or **Hallasan Soju** (Jeju's signature distilled spirit, ~₩5,000). All restaurants in the rotation accept **major cards (Visa/Mastercard) + cash KRW**; bilingual photo menus (English/Chinese) are standard at the cruise-tour-vetted venues. Lunch is **NOT included** in the standard cruise tour pricing — typical out-of-pocket is **₩15,000–25,000 per person** (varies by dish); inclusive lunch upgrade available on booking.
highlights: Traditional Jeju cuisine

visitBasics.hours: Per restaurant operating hours (typically 11:30–14:00 lunch service)
visitBasics.admission: Lunch separate (typically 12,000–25,000 KRW per person; not included in tour price)

convenience.parking: Tour vehicle parks at or near restaurant

smartNotes.tip: Inform guide of any dietary restrictions (vegetarian, halal, allergies) at booking; cash and major cards typically accepted

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The small-group lunch stop gives guests a calmer mid-day break.** A guide-vetted East Jeju restaurant is chosen based on the day's timing, group preference, and opening conditions. The guide can help with menu translation and local dish recommendations, including black pork, seafood, or vegetarian-friendly options when arranged in advance. The longer lunch pace also gives the route a useful buffer if the morning stops take more time for photos or walking.

itineraryStops.name: Seongeup Folk Village
itineraryStops.category: Joseon-era Walled Town — Important Folklore Cultural Heritage (1423 founding)
itineraryStops.description: **Seongeup Folk Village (성읍민속마을)** is an inhabited Joseon-era village that preserves old Jeju stone walls, thatched-roof houses, narrow alleys, and traditional household layouts. Historically, the village served as the administrative center of Jeongui-hyeon, one of the main local districts of Joseon-era Jeju, and its heritage value is recognized as an Important Folklore Cultural Heritage site. Unlike a staged folk park, people still live here, so the visit must be respectful and quiet around private homes. The village is also known for its old zelkova and hackberry trees near the center, traditional Jeju household culture, stone grandfather statues, and local stories connected to village life.
highlights: Traditional thatched-roof houses
Stone wall heritage
Folk demonstrations on select days

visitBasics.admission: Free for general village; some private homes charge for guided visits
visitBasics.walking: Easy — flat dirt and stone paths through the village

smartNotes.photo: Stone walls (dolhareubang grandfathers), thatched-roof houses with smoke holes, and traditional doors are iconic Jeju shots
smartNotes.facilities: Visitor information center at entrance; English maps available
smartNotes.tip: Hire a free volunteer guide at the entrance for cultural context; respect that residents actually live here — don't enter homes uninvited

timeUsed: Main attraction exploration with photo time
itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **Seongeup Folk Village is the main cultural stop of the east route.** In a small group, the guide can explain Jeju stone houses, old village lanes, local household customs, and the role this area played in Joseon-era administration at a more personal pace. Access to private homes or resident-led explanations depends on local conditions, so the visit is presented as a respectful village walk rather than a promised home-entry program.

itineraryStops.name: Return to Jeju Cruise Terminal (Jeju Port — north)
itineraryStops.description: Return drive to the **Jeju International Passenger Terminal** (제주항 국제여객터미널) on Jeju City's northern coast. The route from East Jeju (Seongsan/Seongeup area) takes ~70–80 minutes via Highway 1132 (the coastal ring road) and Route 16, with one designated comfort stop. Drop-off is timed to deliver guests to the terminal **at least 60 minutes before your ship's all-aboard time**, traveler-fit the cruise industry's standard buffer for embarkation queues. The vehicle pulls into the **100-bus parking capacity lot**, guests disembark at the terminal entrance with luggage assistance from the driver/guide. If guests have early evening flights from Jeju International Airport, **Bus #100 connects directly from the cruise terminal to the airport** — the guide can advise on this option. The terminal interior offers convenience store, pharmacy, snack shop, souvenir shops with **tax-free items**, Jeju Bank ATM accepting overseas cards, currency exchange (typical hours 08:00–17:00), and SK Telecom T-mobile Wi-Fi rental counter — useful if guests want last-minute purchases or to reload mobile data before sailaway. **Free shuttle bus to Dongmun Traditional Market** (oldest/largest in Jeju City, ~10 min) is also available for any guests with extra time before all-aboard.

visitBasics.hours: Per drop-off schedule. Please check the listed return time band for your route.

smartNotes.tip: Confirm exact drop-off location with guide before journey begins; cruise tours coordinate with ship departure schedule

itineraryStops.whyOnRoute: **The return drive is kept conversational and flexible.** The guide uses the drive back to answer remaining questions, summarize the day's volcanic and cultural highlights, and monitor timing for the ship. The schedule is planned to return to Jeju Port with a comfortable buffer before all-aboard, with pace adjustments made if traffic or weather changes.

Both variants (Jeju Port north + Gangjeong Port south) enhanced with 1,000+ char descriptions per stop; KB-backed _poi_meta on all attraction stops; matching_profile recalibrated to verified-fact 0-1 float scores; route option-level legacy 0-5 / 0-100 scale fields ALSO normalized to 0-1 float (Phase 1 unification completion for route option scope). _score_audit._methodology: 0-1 float scale per Phase 1 standard. route option-level legacy 0-5/0-100 scale fields ALSO normalized to 0-1 float in this batch (previously the schema unification stopped at top-level matching_profile). Time-weighted indoor_ratio per Fix 1 — computed PER route option then aggregated. Top-level matching_profile uses cross-route option averages weighted toward Gangjeong (the more common 2025+ docking).

unesco_fit.reasoning: VERIFIED HIGH — both variants hit major UNESCO sites. route option 0 (Jeju Port): Seongsan Ilchulbong = TRIPLE UNESCO (World Natural Heritage 2007 + Biosphere Reserve + Global Geopark) + Haenyeo ICH demos. route option 1 (Gangjeong): Hallasan UNESCO World Heritage + Biosphere Reserve + Jusangjeolli Global Geopark. Both variants reliably deliver UNESCO content. 0.85 reflects this consistent delivery. Note: Seongeup is NOT UNESCO (national folklore only). Olle Market and Cheonjiyeon are not UNESCO. Score reflects weighted average of UNESCO density in both variants.
